bugs.description: UserPublic (the API response schema) now declares a `phone` field, but nothing ever populates it: the User table model has no `phone` column and crud never sets one. The API advertises a field that is always null — the response contract no longer matches the data layer.

injection.replace_with:
class UserPublic(UserBase):
    id: uuid.UUID
    created_at: datetime | None = None
    phone: str | None = None

bugs.description: UserCreate now requires a `referral_code` at signup, but it is silently discarded. The User table has no referral_code column, and crud.create_user does `User.model_validate(user_create, ...)`, which drops fields the table model doesn't declare. The API validates referral_code, then loses it on the way to the database.

injection.find:
class UserCreate(UserBase):
    password: str = Field(min_length=8, max_length=128)

bugs.description: UserUpdate renames its `password` field to `new_password`, but crud.update_user still branches on `if "password" in user_data` to hash and persist it. After this rename a password update is silently ignored — the key crud looks for no longer exists, so the new password is never hashed or saved. A change in models.py quietly breaks behavior in crud.py.

injection.find: password: str | None = Field(default=None, min_length=8, max_length=128)
injection.replace_with: new_password: str | None = Field(default=None, min_length=8, max_length=128)

bugs.description: The User table model gains a `last_login` column, but no Alembic migration is added under app/alembic/versions/. Code and database schema drift apart: the column exists in the model but not in the DB, which breaks inserts and queries against the real database at runtime.

injection.replace_with:
class User(UserBase, table=True):
    id: uuid.UUID = Field(default_factory=uuid.uuid4, primary_key=True)
    hashed_password: str
    last_login: datetime | None = None
